content:
{
  "title": "Fact-Anchor Prompting: Interleaving Retrieval-Anchors for Factuality in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often generate hallucinated or factually incorrect statements, especially when responding to open-domain prompts without explicit knowledge grounding.",
  "motivation": "Improving factual reliability is critical for LLM deployment in sensitive domains such as healthcare and law. Current prompting techniques either require external retrieval systems or rely on post-hoc verification, limiting real-time usability and integration. A lightweight, prompt-based intervention could improve factuality without architectural changes or latency overhead.",
  "method": "We propose Fact-Anchor Prompting, a method that interleaves minimal, automatically-extracted knowledge snippets (anchors) directly into user prompts. Given a user query, top-k factual tuples related to the query are retrieved from a structured knowledge base (e.g., Wikidata) using entity linking and relation matching. These anchors are injected into the prompt with special delimiters, instructing the LLM to use them as reference points when generating responses. The prompt template explicitly asks the model to ground its answer in the provided anchors, enabling factual steering without external retrieval APIs during inference.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on FAQt and TruthfulQA for open-domain factuality. Baselines include vanilla prompts, retrieval-augmented generation, and chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics will be factual accuracy (verified by human annotation), hallucination rate, and response fluency. Ablation studies will measure sensitivity to the number, relevance, and formulation of injected anchors."
}
